title: manager-prompt-placeholder-leak
description:
This is a continuation of manager-prompt-placeholder-leak. A high-severity prompt placeholder leak was again reported in a manager_watcher prompt at 2026-05-29T18:54:59.982926+00:00, immediately before the successful manager_watcher run that completed at 2026-05-29T18:55:06.521846+00:00. The current window otherwise appears quiet: manager_watcher, manager_summarizer, and manager_diagnostician all succeeded, with no failed runs, retry clusters, tick activity, queue snapshots, or spend-stream activity recorded.

The impact remains a factory-internal prompt-assembly defect rather than an observed execution failure in this window. The recurrence across multiple prior concerns indicates the issue is persistent rather than cleared, even though the affected run still completed successfully. The evidence in this bundle points most directly to prompt/dispatch plumbing around manager_watcher prompt construction; there is no new evidence here of downstream queue stall or retry amplification.

A long-standing background condition of worktree drift was also noted again, but the watcher note describes that set as unchanged in this window, so it does not appear to be the new or dominant concern. The coherent signal in this bundle is the repeated placeholder leak recurrence without accompanying operational failure data.

evidence.summary_excerpt: The previously escalated high-severity prompt placeholder leak recurred again in a manager_watcher prompt at the start of the window.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"A high-severity placeholder leak recurred in a manager_watcher prompt at 2026-05-29T18:54:59.982926+00:00, continuing the previously escalated prompt-assembly issue."}
manager_watcher run succeeded after the reported leak window; started_at=2026-05-29T18:54:59.983038+00:00 ended_at=2026-05-29T18:55:06.508718+00:00 duration_s=6.526 cost_usd=0.0244155 success=true
{"noteworthy":"Long-standing worktree drift persists: story 33 remains in tests_red and there are many missing-DB worktrees (stories 51-64), but the set appears unchanged in this window."}

escalation_reason: Repeated manager_watcher prompt placeholder leakage has persisted across at least five prior concern intervals and recurred again at 2026-05-29T18:54:59.982926+00:00. L3 judgment is needed because this is a high-clarity factory-internal prompt-assembly/dispatch defect that remains unresolved across consecutive windows, even though immediate downstream harm in this specific window was limited.
